title: Arrays can't be used in sensitivity lists
body:
I tried to compile with `iverilog` the following code:  
```
localparam InCnt = 4;
localparam CntWdt = 16;
reg [CntWdt-1:0] count [InCnt-1:0];
...<some more code here>...

always@(count) begin

...<and more code here>...
```
and I got the following error:  
```
../sv_src/tb_countones_tst.sv:46: error: Array count needs an array index here.
../sv_src/tb_countones_tst.sv:46: error: Failed to evaluate event expression 'count'.
2 error(s) during elaboration.
```

Further details:
* the whole file can be found [here](https://github.com/mzannoni/dig_it_all/blob/0000619b4e37ecb6e8412c5f245b2e23557bcd46/sv_src/tb_countones_tst.sv) (specifically created to test this)
* the command I used: `iverilog -g2012 -o test ../sv_src/tb_countones_tst.sv` (from inside a working sub-dir)

The compilation can be successfully completed (and the simulation run) if the line is corrected by _manually_ expanding explicitly the sensitivity list into the array components:
```
always@(count[0], count[1], count[2], count[3]) begin
```

It seems that Icarus can't accept an array in the sensitivity list. The expected behaviour is that each component of the array is part of the sensitivity list. I verified that this can be correctly understood by `verilator`.

Is this a missing feature that is planned to be implemented?

I think this should be fixed (added?) to address some specific cases, for instance:
* if the array is very large (the actual use case where I needed it would require me to manually write down 32 signals in the sensitivity list...) then a lot of manual coding (and related error-prone code duplication) is required
* if the array is defined with a parameter, it is essentially not possible to implement this (or is there a way to write an array expansion in a sensitivity list?)
